llm_instruction: Use this file only after a candidate job was selected. Copy the BASF URL exactly from job.url; never construct job links.

job.title: Supervisor, Sales, Standard Surfactants, South China
job.description: About The Role • Manage sales and customers and achieve the sales and profit targets • Support business development by identifying sales potentials, new customers and applications • Research and update market intelligence to supervisor/team How You Will Do It 1. Sales customer management • Plan and manage EMO (Oleo Chemicals Alcohols) business including key accounts and direct accounts/dealers/distributors to meet sales target; ensure account strategies are exploited consistently including new opportunities. • Report all opportunities into Salesforce process at least one month in advance. • Maximize profitability by contributing to the full utilization of assets in Asia Pacific and not limited by local view • Enhance account relationships and meet sales targets for each assigned account and as per KPI’s via replacement or project activities including calling and visiting existing/prospective customers to fully understand and identify their needs to create a long-term customer supplier relationship. • Timely response to customer requests including product information, technical support via coordination with technical, customer services, logistics, Regional Pricing and Margin Management, regulatory affairs, etc as necessary to solve customer problem/NCM and to enhance customer service and satisfaction 2. Sales performance • Develop strong client relationships over time that provide significant inputs to product planning, price and service strategies, manage and share relevant information, and update in systems • Oversee order processes and manage demand forecasts, ensuring accuracy and timely execution. • Manage customer complaints and supply chain related issues effectively to ensure satisfaction and operational efficiency. • Consolidate data and prepare analysis e.g. customer needs, including product application, sales potentials, services requirements, buying cycles, delivery schedules and internal regulations like pricing structure for defined, customer credibility/profiles to help secure/enhance business opportunities and identify future customers. 3. Contract and payment receivable management • Coordinate with the personnel of customer services, logistics and inventory control to support the delivery of sales. Carry out pricing and payment policies as per strategies and advice given by Regional Pricing and Margin Management and update database of customer creditability according to company credit policies. • Trace the customers’ payment process to...
